# Alex Gerlitz - Python Backend Automation Engineer

> I build backend-owned AI workflow and operations systems for real businesses: RAG, transcript and call analysis, approval flows, CRM/ERP/API adapters, FastAPI/PostgreSQL backends, Docker/CI, runbooks, and recovery paths.

This is the AI-readable review index for my public GitHub/LinkedIn route. It is intentionally short: start with the strongest review path, then inspect the repositories and verification routes that show how the work is built, tested, operated, and handed off.

## Primary Review Route

- [Delivery Capability](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/delivery-capability.html): role-market fit for AI-assisted Python automation, internal tools, backend integration, QA Automation Python bridge, Docker/Compose boundary, parser-readable keywords, first useful workflow slice, verification, and handoff.
- [Application Fit Pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/application-fit-pack.html): parser-readable role application lanes for Python backend/internal tools, Integration/API/CRM, QA Automation Python, AI workflow automation, exact stack keywords, role-specific work samples, and first application message shape.
- [Application Outreach Pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/application-outreach-pack.html): role-specific application and recruiter-message layer with first-person outreach blocks, ATS keyword bundles, vacancy triage, truthful Docker/CI and AI boundaries, privacy limits, and work samples for Python backend, QA/API Python, integration/API/CRM, and AI workflow roles.
- [DriveDesk AI Operator](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/drivedesk-proof-route.html): backend-owned AI workflow evidence for RAG, transcript analysis, privacy redaction, lead scoring, approvals, CRM handoff, audit, retries, and adapter boundaries.
- [First Backend Role Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/first-backend-role.html): first-role Python/backend bridge for Junior Backend Developer, Junior Python Developer, Back End Developer, Internal Tools Developer, and Integration Developer matches, with bounded tasks, reviewable diffs, tests or smoke checks, docs, privacy boundary, and public-safe Autoschool work sample.
- [Backend Role Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/backend-first-screen.html): fast backend/internal-tools fit for Back End Developer, Python Developer, Backend Engineer, Internal Tools Engineer, and Integration Engineer roles, using Autoschool intake/admin work sample, the synthetic demo seed, DriveDesk Core, skill evidence, Decision-Ready Contact, and PDF resume.
- [Autoschool Intake/Admin work sample](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/autoschool-intake-admin.html): public-safe first-job evidence for Telegram request intake, backend validation, database record, admin queue handoff, operator status workflow, and privacy boundary without exposing learner data or live admin screenshots.
- [AI Backend Review Pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/ai-backend-proof-pack.html): compact route through Python/backend automation, AI workflow, integration, Docker/CI handoff, live evidence, verification, Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume, and resume evidence. It also exposes structured review order, role shortlist, and offer-fit data.
- [AI Automation Role Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/ai-automation-role-fit.html): focused role-fit route for backend-owned AI workflows, RAG/context retrieval, structured outputs, approvals, CRM/API handoff, tests, logs, docs, and supportable runbooks.
- [Enterprise Readiness](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/enterprise-readiness.html): deeper employer-grade review route for architecture, state ownership, integration contracts, English-first docs, async review paths, specialist review evidence, backend-owned AI workflow signal, reliability, privacy, observability, CI, runbooks, Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume, and handoff quality. It also exposes structured enterprise-readiness data for decision path, specialist signal, evidence matrix, and next review targets.
- [Role Fit Pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/application-pack.html): role-fit summary for Python/backend automation, internal tools, API/CRM integration, QA Automation Python, Autoschool intake/admin first-job evidence, AI workflow automation, reliability handoff, Europe-compatible remote signal, shortlist evidence, structured role-fit decision path, and Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume.
- [DriveDesk Flagship Backend Workflow](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/flagship-platform.html): larger backend/internal-tools work sample around roles, records, workflows, adapters, audit/outbox, admin/operator surfaces, Docker/CI handoff, observability, backups, and recovery paths.
- [Hiring decision route](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/hiring-decision.html): one-minute role fit, high-bar specialist screen, why to shortlist me, specialist reason to message, risk reduction, strongest evidence, first-month ownership, Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume, structured 30-second screen, shortlist scorecard data, and specialist-reason data.
- [LinkedIn profile signal](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/linkedin-proof-signal.html): compact public update route with verified live skill surface, Delivery Capability, Application Fit Pack, first Featured work sample, DriveDesk AI Operator, AI Backend Review Pack, Decision-Ready Contact, profile-view signal, and initial contact direction. It also exposes the next LinkedIn skill-order target and a LinkedIn Activation Pack.
- [LinkedIn Recruiter Packet](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/linkedin-recruiter-packet.html): short recruiter decision page for role lane, first work sample, scope boundaries, contact prompt, and first assignment; first-fit fields are role lane, first work sample, scope boundaries, and contact prompt.
- [Decision-Ready Contact](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/decision-ready-contact.html): fastest message route, recruiter offer triage, minimum first context, first contact options, fit read, risk check, smallest responsible first slice, scoped offer route, next step, structured contact-route data, and best first review links.
- [Recruiter review pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/output/pdf/alex-gerlitz-recruiter-proof-pack.pdf): two-page role-fit handoff with first-month ownership, work samples, truthful Docker/CI and AI boundaries, contact route, and public-safe privacy boundary.
- [LinkedIn banner asset](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/assets/linkedin-banner.png): prepared 1584x396 top-card cover image for the DriveDesk AI Operator profile route, with Python/backend automation, AI workflow, CRM/ERP integration, review path, live smoke, PDF resume, and GitHub CI signals.
- [PDF resume](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/output/pdf/alex-gerlitz-python-backend-automation-resume.pdf): one-page application handoff with Python/backend automation, internal tools, API/CRM integration, QA Automation Python, Autoschool intake/admin work sample, public work samples, AI-native delivery loop, Europe-compatible async signal, review-by-intent routes, and contact paths.
- [AI Ops Hiring Signal Brief](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/ai-ops-workflow-kit/blob/main/docs/evidence/hiring-signal-brief.txt): shortest Python/backend evidence file for RAG quality, approval, CRM-safe handoff, retry/dead-letter behavior, idempotency, dry-run boundaries, and public-safe evidence.
- [AI Ops Employer Trigger Evidence](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/ai-ops-workflow-kit/blob/main/docs/EMPLOYER_TRIGGER_PROOF.md): maps employer triggers to concrete repo evidence, verification commands, first-result evidence, and backend-owned workflow boundaries.
- [AI Ops demo walkthrough](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/ai-ops-workflow-kit/blob/main/docs/DEMO_WALKTHROUGH.md): committed visual route for transcript -> RAG -> approval -> CRM-safe handoff.
- [Recruiter Preferences](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/recruiter-preferences.html): Open-to-Work alignment for job titles, remote work mode, employment types, recruiter search keywords, recruiter filter map, evidence stack by search intent, review order, Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume, and first contact fit. It also maps the LinkedIn skills search surface to public evidence and exposes structured data for recruiter/search tooling.
- [Start conversation](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/start-conversation.html): recruiter shortcut, first-contact route, message triggers, role-target links, PDF resume, fixed-scope project offer route, ownership-first context, and the fastest contact path.
- [Contact routes](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/inbound-response.html): message triggers, search intent first links for AI Automation/RAG, Python/backend/internal tools, CRM/ERP/API integration, reliability/Docker handoff, fixed-scope offers, plus decision-ready context and response shape.
- [Selected work-sample projects](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/projects.html): repo-level evidence across DriveDesk AI Operator, DriveDesk Core, AI Ops Workflow Kit, DeployMate, and MPlusForm.
- [Remote role targets](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/role-targets.html): search-fit roles mapped to evidence, first-month ownership, direct review routes, and structured recruiter search snapshot data.
- [Work with me](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/work-with-me.html): remote role and fixed-scope project route with best immediate starts, fit filter, evidence links, and contact path.
- [Proposal-ready Services request](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/services.html): LinkedIn Services request shape with offer router, current workflow, systems involved, one observable success condition, constraints, what must not break, Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume, and services decision-route structured data.
- [Skill evidence map](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/skill-evidence.html): skills mapped to public evidence for Python/backend automation, AI automation, CRM/ERP integration, QA/API verification, Docker/CI boundary, and AI-native delivery discipline.
- [Verification pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/verification-pack.html): CI, live smoke, public demo, evidence links, evidence freshness, current green evidence, and structured technical verification data.
- [AI Backend Review Pack Markdown](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/AI_BACKEND_PROOF_PACK.md): GitHub-readable review order for recruiters, hiring managers, and technical reviewers, with Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume.
- [Enterprise Readiness Markdown](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/ENTERPRISE_READINESS.md): GitHub-readable matrix for enterprise Python/backend, integration, reliability, privacy, and handoff review signals, Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume, and the structured review data exposed by the HTML route.
- [LinkedIn Services Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/linkedin-service-page-fit.html): public HTML fit filter for broad LinkedIn Services labels around Python/backend automation, AI workflow, integration, data, Docker/CI reliability, internal operations, and Decision-Ready Contact before PDF resume. It also exposes structured broad-category, best-fit request, not-fit service filter, and services decision-route data.

## Employer Decision Path

- 2-minute fit check: Delivery Capability -> Application Fit Pack -> Application Outreach Pack -> Enterprise Readiness -> Role Fit Pack -> Decision-Ready Contact -> PDF resume.
- 10-minute technical review: AI Ops reviewer snapshot, DriveDesk Core, and verification pack.
- 30-minute specialist review: privacy boundary, live owner evidence, DeployMate evidence, current CI/smoke routes, and handoff evidence.

## Best-Fit Work

- Remote-only Python/backend automation, internal tools, API/CRM integration, QA Automation Python, AI workflow automation, and reliability roles for international and distributed teams.
- Fixed-scope projects with one messy workflow, one success condition, and a first working slice with tests, logs, docs, and a handoff path.
- Strongest fit: backend-owned AI workflows, CRM/ERP/API adapters, RAG systems, Telegram/operator approvals, internal admin tools, deployment/recovery workflows, and production-style evidence.
- LinkedIn Services fit filter: Web Development or Custom Software Development requests are a fit only when they include Python/backend automation, AI workflow, integration, Docker/CI reliability, or internal-operations ownership.
- Not my target right now: onsite-only work, pure prompt/content tasks, isolated brochure/static websites, student/course assignments, generic mobile/ecommerce apps without backend or integration ownership, and vague work where success cannot be verified.

## Search-Fit Role Targets

- Headline: Python Backend Automation Engineer | DriveDesk, Internal Tools, API/CRM Integrations, QA Automation Python, RAG Workflow Evidence, FastAPI, Docker.
- Best-fit titles: Junior Backend Developer, Junior Python Developer, Back End Developer, Python Developer, Python Automation Engineer, Backend Automation Engineer, Internal Tools Developer, Integration Engineer, API Integration Engineer, CRM Integration Engineer, QA Automation Python, Test Automation Engineer, and Support Engineer with Python. AI workflow and RAG work are evidence-backed differentiators, not the first title I need to be screened under.
- LinkedIn recruiter search alignment: remote-only full-time roles where Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Docker, GitHub Actions, RAG, Vector Databases, CRM/ERP/API integration, QA Automation Python, OpenAPI, n8n, Telegram, or pgvector appear in the role.
- Work mode: remote-only full-time roles or concrete fixed-scope projects with Python/backend automation, internal tools, API/CRM integration, QA Automation Python, AI workflow automation, or reliability handoff.
- First-month ownership: ship one business workflow slice with backend-owned state, tests, logs, docs, deployment path, and handoff.

## Pinned Evidence Repositories

- [DriveDesk Core](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/drivedesk-core): FastAPI/PostgreSQL operations backend foundation with tenants, RBAC, audit/outbox, adapters, OpenAPI, Docker, pytest, CI, docs, and public demo.
- [Autoschool Intake/Admin work sample](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/autoschool-intake-admin.html): sanitized route for a private driving-school bot/admin workflow where public evidence uses synthetic data only.
- [AI Ops Workflow Kit](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/ai-ops-workflow-kit): hiring signal brief, document intake, committed demo GIF/walkthrough, privacy redaction before RAG/approval/CRM handoff, deterministic RAG quality eval, RAG retrieval, transcript analysis, approvals, Telegram callback, dry-run Bitrix CRM handoff, retry/dead-letter behavior, idempotent outbox, reviewer observability snapshot, 54-pass public verification gate, CI, and smoke checks.
- [DeployMate](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/deploymate): self-hosted Docker deployment control panel evidence with FastAPI, Next.js, PostgreSQL, SSH runtime tooling, health checks, logs, release gates, and runbooks.
- [MPlusForm](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/MPlusForm): validation-boundary and trust-model automation backend evidence with untrusted local files, optional Python sync, server-approved snapshots, Windows operations docs, Lua addon boundary, and [public verification run](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/MPlusForm/actions/runs/28296918568).

## Fast Contact

- Message me first when there is one messy workflow, one risky integration boundary, or one Python/backend automation, API/CRM, QA automation, AI workflow, or reliability slice that should become testable, logged, documented, and handed off.
- First reply promise: fit read, risky assumptions, smallest responsible first slice, review path to inspect, and the right next route: remote role, fixed-scope project, technical review, or no-fit.
- First-contact prompts cover remote role screens, workflow projects, and technical reviews.
- Message prompt anchors: "Can you send the best review path and the smallest first slice you would own?", "Can you map the risks, review path, and first responsible slice?", and "strongest repo, CI/live-smoke evidence".
- Inbound offer fit: best messages name one remote Python/backend automation role, internal tools role, API/CRM integration boundary, QA automation path, AI workflow, or reliability need plus one success condition, systems involved, ownership boundary, timeline, and compensation band if shareable.
- Fastest recruiter route: Message on LinkedIn -> Recruiter review pack -> PDF resume -> First Backend Role Fit -> Autoschool Intake/Admin work sample.
- First useful result: a verified slice with backend-owned state, tests, logs, docs, and a handoff route.
- Message reason router: use Start conversation to choose remote role hiring, workflow/project, or technical review before sending LinkedIn context.
- Recruiter shortcut: send role title, remote setup, stack, first-month ownership, timeline, and compensation band if shareable. Best match: Python/backend automation, internal tools, API/CRM integration, QA Automation Python, AI workflow automation, or reliability handoff.

- [Remote AI Automation Services](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/services.html): fast first message and proposal-ready route for one remote role or one messy workflow, one success condition, systems involved, constraints, what must not break, Decision-Ready Contact, and a working-slice outcome.
- [Remote role targets](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/role-targets.html)

- [Decision-Ready Contact](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/decision-ready-contact.html)
- [Application Outreach Pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/application-outreach-pack.html)
- [Inbound brief](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/intake-brief.html): role, project, offer route, success condition, systems involved, constraints, and verification context.
- [Contact routes](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/inbound-response.html)
- [PDF resume](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/output/pdf/alex-gerlitz-python-backend-automation-resume.pdf)

## Review Notes

- AI-native delivery loop: AI tooling compresses discovery, implementation, debugging, docs, and review while architecture, state boundaries, privacy checks, tests, logs, deployment, runbooks, and shipped quality stay engineering-owned.
- The main signal is not no-code node wiring. The signal is backend-owned state, deterministic RAG quality eval, privacy boundaries, audit, retries, idempotency, integration contracts, smoke checks, and runbooks.
